title: ProtocolLab Specification Coverage Contract Verification
scope: Verify the specification document, requirement, catalog, scenario mapping, named profile, optional run-plan selection, executor capability discovery, and optional coverage-evidence sidecar contracts and their compatibility with existing public ProtocolLab contracts.
verification_method: Repository inspection, JSON Schema validation, positive and negative fixture validation, referential integrity checks, exact scenario-check resolution, digest requirements, compatibility review, traceability review, and claim-language scanning.
preconditions: The public repository remains contract-only and language-neutral.
Scenario v1 and Evidence Report v1 remain unchanged in the first contract slice.
The RFC 9000 pilot catalog and mappings remain proposed rather than approved implementation claims.
procedure: Parse every JSON contract, fixture, specification record, and SpecTrace artifact.
Validate all valid specification fixtures and confirm every invalid fixture fails for its intended reason.
Confirm each requirement references a known document and exact source locator.
Confirm each catalog path and ID resolves and declares explicit completeness scope.
Confirm each mapping references an exact root scenario and known validation check IDs.
Confirm validates and negative-validates relationships require check IDs and evidence types.
Confirm coverage profiles reference known catalog requirements, scenarios, roles, and relationships.
Confirm coverage evidence requires catalog, mapping, profile where used, scenario-package, cell, and artifact digests.
Confirm a run-level index supports multiple catalog-specific sidecars while pinning every sidecar digest.
Confirm scenario-pack coverage metadata resolves package-relative catalog, requirement, mapping, profile, scenario, and check records.
Confirm structured validation artifacts require explicit nonempty check outcomes and never infer them from overall status.
Confirm run-plan coverage selections pin catalog and mapping snapshots and identify at least one profile, requirement, or check target.
Confirm executor structured-outcome declarations retain exact schema, check, authority, compatibility, artifact, and unsupported-reason metadata without becoming evidence.
Confirm legacy scenario, run-plan, evidence-report, package, and artifact fixtures remain valid.
Confirm no public artifact claims universal compliance, certification, or whole-RFC coverage from the pilot catalog.
Confirm traceability links resolve reciprocally across SPEC, ARC, WI, VER, schemas, and fixtures.
expected_result: The public repository exposes coherent, backward-compatible specification coverage contracts with exact source and mapping provenance, honest relationship semantics, check-level proof requirements, and no implementation conformance or universal-score inference.
status_summary: Passed on 2026-07-12. JSON Schema, positive and negative fixtures, document and requirement identity, catalog paths, exact scenario and check references, named-profile references, package-relative mapping snapshots, optional run-plan selection, executor capability discovery, digest requirements, compatibility boundaries, documentation links, and repository traceability all passed the repository-health validation.
